event.title: Mass Funeral Ceremonies for Former Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
event.summary: Iran staged mass funeral ceremonies over six days for former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed in joint US-Israeli strikes in February. Millions gathered in Tehran for processions, while his successor son Mojtaba Khamenei was conspicuously absent amid reported security concerns about Israeli assassination.

related_events.title: State Funeral of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei

sources.ownership: Publicly funded; governed by the BBC Royal Charter. Funded primarily through the UK television licence fee paid by UK households.

claims.text: Ali Khamenei was killed in joint US-Israeli strikes at the start of a war in February 2026, aged 86, after ruling Iran since 1989.
claims.rationale: All sources across center-left and center-right outlets consistently report that Khamenei was killed in US-Israeli strikes in February, meeting the cross-spectrum threshold for 'agreed'.

framing_variants.characterization: Ruled the Islamic Republic from 1989 until his death in February; killed in joint US-Israeli strikes.
Killed in joint US-Israeli strikes at the start of the war.
Killed by the first airstrike of the war launched by the US and Israel; 37-year reign ended in February.
86-year-old cleric who ruled Iran for decades; family members also killed in a February 28 airstrike.
Killed aged 86 on the first day of the US-Israeli war with Iran on 28 February.
Assassinated by Israeli jet planes; 37-year reign; aged 86.
Killed in February during the first airstrike when the US and Israel started the current war.
Killed more than four months ago in US and Israeli strikes at the start of the war.

claims.text: Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba Khamenei succeeded him as Supreme Leader of Iran.
claims.rationale: Center-left outlets (BBC, Guardian) and center-right outlets (Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, Euronews) all confirm Mojtaba Khamenei's succession as Supreme Leader.

framing_variants.characterization: His second son and successor, Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.
Iran's new Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei.
New supreme leader — son of Ali Khamenei.
The country's current Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.
Mojtaba Khamenei, who succeeded his father.
He was succeeded by his son, Mojtaba Khamenei.

claims.text: Iran staged mass funeral ceremonies for Ali Khamenei spanning six days and multiple cities across Iran and Iraq, with millions gathering in Tehran.
claims.rationale: Sources across center-left and center-right outlets uniformly report the large-scale, multi-day, multi-city nature of the funeral ceremonies.

framing_variants.characterization: Official funeral proceedings began on Friday; Iranian authorities say 12–20 million expected; body lying in state at Grand Mosalla.
Iranian state media say six days of ceremonies span several locations across Iran and neighbouring Iraq.
Iran staging mass funeral processions; thousands gathered at Grand Mosalla; streets full by early morning.
Sea of mourners flooded Tehran for procession; crowd appeared larger than 2020 Soleimani procession of over 1 million.
Six-day, five-city funeral; organisers claim up to 30 million may attend at some point.
Millions assembled in the capital for the six-day, five-city funeral.

claims.text: Khamenei's coffin was displayed alongside those of family members killed in the strikes, including a young granddaughter.
claims.rationale: Multiple outlets across center-left and center-right confirm the display of family members' coffins alongside Khamenei's; sources differ slightly on the granddaughter's age (one year old vs. 14 months).

framing_variants.characterization: Coffin displayed alongside those of four relatives killed in the strikes, including one-year-old granddaughter Zahra Mohammadi Golpayegani.
Coffins of members of his family killed in a February 28 airstrike displayed alongside his.
Other members of his family killed in the raid, including his 14-month-old granddaughter.
Coffins of Khamenei and family members also killed in the strikes.

claims.text: The funeral procession's planned itinerary included movement through Tehran on Monday, Qom on Tuesday, Shia sites in Iraq on Wednesday, and burial in Mashhad on Thursday.
claims.rationale: Center-left and center-right outlets consistently report the multi-city itinerary culminating in burial at Mashhad on Thursday.

framing_variants.characterization: Coffin moved to Qom Tuesday, significant Shia site in Iraq Wednesday, burial in northeast hometown Mashhad Thursday.
Late ayatollah buried in hometown of Mashhad on Thursday.
Burial at Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad, his birthplace; coffins on 12-hour journey to Mehrabad Airport.
At request of Iraqi politicians, body carried through Iraqi Shia cities of Karbala and Najaf.
Coffins will make 12-hour journey to Mehrabad International Airport; procession ends Thursday with burial at Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad, where he was born.

claims.text: Mojtaba Khamenei was conspicuously absent from his father's funeral on Sunday.
claims.rationale: Both center-left (BBC) and center-right (Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post) outlets note Mojtaba Khamenei's conspicuous absence from the funeral.

framing_variants.characterization: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was conspicuously absent from his father's funeral; has not appeared in public since his appointment in early March.
His second son and successor, Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, was conspicuously absent from his father's funeral.
Has not been seen in public since he was injured; father, wife, and son were killed in opening strikes.
Seriously wounded during the targeted killing of his father; was said to be afraid to attend.

claims.text: Iranian security officials reportedly rejected Mojtaba Khamenei's request to attend the funeral, fearing Israeli assassination; separately, he was described as afraid to attend and as having not appeared in public since being injured.
claims.rationale: Both the Times of Israel and Jerusalem Post (both center-right) report the security rejection and injury, but no center-left outlet independently confirms these specific details, so cross-spectrum agreement is not established.

framing_variants.characterization: According to Sunday reports, Iranian security officials rejected his request to attend, fearing Israeli assassination; father, wife, and son were killed in opening strikes.
Seriously wounded during the targeted killing of his father; was said to be afraid to attend; many other senior officials also chose not to attend and sent representatives instead.

claims.text: Ali Khamenei's other three sons — Masoud, Mostafa, and Meysam — attended the funeral service on Sunday.
claims.rationale: Two BBC articles (both center-left) confirm the attendance of the three other sons by name; no cross-spectrum source independently verifies this detail.

framing_variants.characterization: Ali Khamenei's other three sons — Masoud, Mostafa and Meysam — all attended the service on Sunday.
Ali Khamenei's other three sons — Masoud, Mostafa and Meysam — all attended the service held in the capital on Sunday.

claims.text: Iranian authorities reported over 4,000 people visited medical centres near the Grand Mosalla; official media warned of crowd crush risks; strict security measures were imposed.
claims.rationale: BBC and Times of Israel both report safety measures and crowd management concerns; the medical figure is attributed to Iran's official news agency IRNA.

framing_variants.characterization: Iran's official news agency Irna reported more than 4,000 people visited medical centres; no deaths recorded; strict security measures imposed; official media warned of crowd crush risk.

Authorities on loudspeakers urged public to walk slowly, not push, stay to edges; authorities hoping to avoid repeat of chaos at Khomeini's 1989 funeral which killed more than 10 and injured over 10,000.

claims.text: During funeral ceremonies, attendees called for the murder of Donald Trump; a poet said 'Trump's murder is our responsibility'; mourners held banners reading 'kill Trump' and 'kill Netanyahu'; an effigy of Trump being hanged was seen along the procession route.
claims.rationale: Center-left (BBC, Guardian) and center-right (Times of Israel) outlets all report anti-Trump statements and imagery at the funeral, meeting the cross-spectrum threshold.

framing_variants.characterization: Poet Mohammad Rasouli said 'Trump's murder is our responsibility'; crowds chanted 'death to America' and 'death to Israel'; mourners held banners reading 'kill Trump' and 'kill Bibi'.
An effigy of Trump being hanged was seen along the procession route; mourners waved Hezbollah flags and red flags symbolising revenge.
During two days of public farewell ceremonies, there were calls for the murder of Donald Trump.

claims.text: Trump said peace talks with Iran had been paused for a week for the funeral; he stated the US could eliminate many Iranian senior officials in one military action but would not do so because it would leave no one to negotiate with.
claims.rationale: BBC (center-left) and The Guardian (center-left) both report Trump's statements about the pause in talks and his stated reasoning; these outlets span within center-left but are different outlets. However, for cross-spectrum confirmation, we note the Times of Israel also references ongoing US-Iran negotiations context.

framing_variants.characterization: News website Axios quoted Trump saying talks were paused for a week for the funeral; the US could take out Iranian officials in 'one shot' but won't because 'we would have nobody to negotiate with.'
Trump said the US will either make a deal with Iran or 'finish the job'; told reporters he would rather make a deal.

claims.text: Trump expressed surprise at Iranians crying at the funeral, suggested mourning may be genuine or 'fake tears,' while a mourner responded that their grief was not fake.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports Trump's 'fake tears' remark and the mourner Zahra Safaei's direct rebuttal in detail.

framing_variants.characterization: Trump said he was surprised to see Iranians crying and suggested 'Maybe it's fake tears'; mourner Zahra Safaei, 50, responded: 'We did not make a revolution 47 years ago to shed fake tears.'

claims.text: A ceasefire between Iran and Israel is currently holding while talks on a permanent peace deal continue.
claims.rationale: BBC (center-left) and Jerusalem Post (center-right) both reference an ongoing ceasefire and continuing negotiations between Iran and Israel.

framing_variants.characterization: A fragile ceasefire between the warring countries is currently holding while talks on a permanent peace deal continue.
Israel is not directly or indirectly involved in the US-Iran negotiations; IDF continues to prepare for a scenario in which fighting resumes.

claims.text: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz stated that Khamenei 'was assassinated by Israel' because he 'set in motion and led the plan to destroy Israel,' and vowed that future Iranian leaders who try to harm Israel will face the same fate.
claims.rationale: Both Times of Israel and Jerusalem Post (both center-right) report Katz's statements; no center-left outlet independently reports these quotes, so cross-spectrum confirmation is not established.

framing_variants.characterization: Katz said Khamenei 'was assassinated by Israel because he set in motion and led the plan to destroy Israel'; the US-Israeli war 'removed the immediate threat of Israel's destruction and significantly damaged Iran's strategic capabilities'; Jerusalem 'prepared to once again defend itself on its own, at any time and against any threat.'
Days earlier, Katz said the current Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was 'marked for death.'

claims.text: Katz's stated justification for Israel's assassination of Khamenei was that Khamenei had set in motion and led a plan to destroy Israel.
claims.rationale: Both center-right outlets report Katz's stated rationale linking the assassination to Khamenei's alleged plan against Israel; no cross-spectrum source independently carries the justification.

framing_variants.characterization: Khamenei 'was assassinated by Israel because he set in motion and led the plan to destroy Israel.'
IDF believes the war exacted a heavy price from Iran; Israeli officials believe the regime and IRGC are not close to collapse but have begun rehabilitation.

claims.text: Iran has repeatedly denied plotting to kill Trump, while US federal authorities have been tracking Iranian threats against Trump and other US officials for years, and hardline Iranian propaganda footage has long suggested Trump was in Tehran's crosshairs.
claims.rationale: Iran's official denial of assassination plots against Trump stands in direct contradiction to the US government's tracking of Iranian threats and evidence from Iranian propaganda; the Times of Israel reports both accounts.

framing_variants.characterization: Iran has repeatedly denied plotting to kill Trump; US federal authorities have been tracking Iranian threats stemming from Trump's 2020 ordering of Soleimani's killing; hardline propaganda footage has long suggested Trump was in Tehran's crosshairs.

claims.text: International media organisations operating in Iran are restricted from using their material on BBC's Persian Service; these restrictions apply to all international media operating in Iran.
claims.rationale: Multiple BBC articles (all center-left) consistently disclose this restriction, framing it as a condition applied broadly to all international media in Iran.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC imposed a condition that Lyse Doucet's material cannot be used on the BBC's Persian Service; restrictions apply to all international media organisations operating in Iran.
BBC's chief international correspondent reporting from Tehran on condition that none of her material is used on the BBC's Persian Service; restrictions apply to all international media.
Al Maghafi's reporting restricted from use on BBC's Persian Service; restrictions apply to all international media organisations operating in Iran.

claims.text: British and American journalists in Iran were officially advised not to speak to mourners at the funeral.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports this specific guidance to British and American journalists not to speak to mourners.

framing_variants.characterization: British and American journalists have officially been advised not to speak to the mourners.

claims.text: Senior Iranian officials including President Masoud Pezeshkian, IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi, and Quds Force head Esmail Qaani attended the funeral and procession.
claims.rationale: BBC (center-left), Jerusalem Post (center-right), and The Guardian (center-left) all confirm attendance of senior Iranian officials, spanning the political spectrum.

framing_variants.characterization: Senior regime figures including President Masoud Pezeshkian and Revolutionary Guards chief Ahmad Vahidi attended.
IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi attended; Israeli security establishment monitored the event.
New IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi attended the funeral and procession; Esmail Qaani, who leads the Quds Force overseeing foreign operations, also attended.

claims.text: Former Iranian president Mahmoud Ahmadinejad was seen attending the funeral procession, in what appeared to be his first major public appearance since fighting broke out with the US and Israel.
claims.rationale: The Times of Israel (center-right) and The Guardian (center-left) both report Ahmadinejad's attendance, constituting cross-spectrum agreement.

framing_variants.characterization: Former president Mahmoud Ahmadinejad was seen attending the procession, according to local media.
Former president Mahmoud Ahmadinejad was seen during the day in what seems to be his first major public appearance since fighting broke out with the US and Israel.

claims.text: Hamas is dissolving the body that has governed Gaza since 2007 and handing over control to a technocratic committee — the National Committee for the Administration of Gaza (NCAG) — established under Trump's Board of Peace as part of an October 2025 ceasefire deal.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports this Hamas governance transition announcement in detail within this article cluster.

framing_variants.characterization: Hamas is dissolving the body that governed Gaza since 2007 and handing control to a technocratic NCAG committee; Hamas spokesman Hazem Qassem said this removes pretexts for 'the occupation'; Board of Peace said assessment will be guided by actions, not promises, and requires consolidation of all weapons under NCAG.

claims.text: Israeli Netanyahu urged the US not to sell F-35 jets or components to Turkey, arguing it would upset the power balance in the region, as Trump traveled to Ankara for a NATO summit.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports Netanyahu's objection to F-35 sales to Turkey in this article cluster.

framing_variants.characterization: Netanyahu urged the US not to sell F-35s or components to Turkey, saying Erdogan 'calls openly for the annihilation of Israel'; warned it would 'upset the power balance' in the Middle East; Trump traveled to Ankara for a NATO summit.

claims.text: An Israeli drone strike on a car in south Lebanon killed four people including three women, despite a fragile Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire; Israel has maintained intermittent strikes on south Lebanon, citing targeting of Hezbollah sites.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian in this cluster reports the south Lebanon car strike and its details.

framing_variants.characterization: Lebanese state media said an Israeli drone struck a car in south Lebanon killing four including three women; victims were returning from inspecting their family home in Nabatieh al-Fawqa; Israel usually says it targets Hezbollah sites; both sides accuse each other of ceasefire violations; Lebanon's president said Israeli occupation prevents Lebanese army deployment.

paragraphs.text: Iran staged mass funeral ceremonies for former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed in joint US-Israeli strikes in February 2026 at age 86. The ceremonies spanned six days and multiple cities across Iran and Iraq, with millions gathering in Tehran for processions honoring the leader who had ruled Iran since 1989.

Khamenei's son Mojtaba succeeded him as Supreme Leader following the February strikes. The succession occurred in the context of a war that began with the US-Israeli operation that killed the elder Khamenei.

The funeral procession's planned itinerary moved through Tehran on Monday, Qom on Tuesday, Shia sites in Iraq on Wednesday, with burial scheduled in Mashhad on Thursday. Khamenei's coffin was displayed alongside those of family members killed in the same strikes, including a young granddaughter.

Senior Iranian officials attended the funeral and procession, including President Masoud Pezeshkian, IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi, and Quds Force head Esmail Qaani. Former president Mahmoud Ahmadinejad was also seen at the procession, in what appeared to be his first major public appearance since fighting broke out with the US and Israel.

A ceasefire between Iran and Israel is currently holding while talks on a permanent peace deal continue. The negotiations represent ongoing diplomatic efforts following the February conflict that killed Khamenei.

The new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, was conspicuously absent from his father's funeral on Sunday. His three brothers — Masoud, Mostafa, and Meysam — attended the funeral service, according to center-left outlets.

Center-right Israeli outlets reported that Iranian security officials rejected Mojtaba Khamenei's request to attend the funeral, fearing Israeli assassination. These outlets further reported that he had been injured and had not appeared in public since, though these specific details were not independently confirmed by center-left sources covering the event.

During the funeral ceremonies, attendees called for the murder of US President Donald Trump. A poet declared "Trump's murder is our responsibility," while mourners held banners reading "kill Trump" and "kill Netanyahu." An effigy of Trump being hanged was seen along the procession route.

Center-left outlets reported that Trump said peace talks with Iran had been paused for a week for the funeral. He stated the US could eliminate many Iranian senior officials in one military action but would not do so because it would leave no one to negotiate with.

Only the BBC reported Trump expressing surprise at Iranians crying at the funeral, suggesting the mourning may be genuine or "fake tears." A mourner identified as Zahra Safaei responded that their grief was not fake.

Center-right Israeli outlets reported statements by Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz that Khamenei "was assassinated by Israel" because he "set in motion and led the plan to destroy Israel." Katz vowed that future Iranian leaders who try to harm Israel will face the same fate. These specific quotes from Katz were not independently reported by center-left outlets covering the funeral.

The Times of Israel reported that Iran has repeatedly denied plotting to kill Trump, while US federal authorities have been tracking Iranian threats against Trump and other US officials for years. Hardline Iranian propaganda footage has long suggested Trump was in Tehran's crosshairs, according to the outlet.

Iranian authorities reported over 4,000 people visited medical centers near the Grand Mosalla, according to Iran's official news agency IRNA. Official media warned of crowd crush risks, and strict security measures were imposed during the ceremonies.

Multiple BBC reports disclosed that international media organizations operating in Iran are restricted from using their material on BBC's Persian Service, with these restrictions applying to all international media operating in Iran. The Guardian separately reported that British and American journalists in Iran were officially advised not to speak to mourners at the funeral.

In related regional developments, The Guardian reported that Hamas is dissolving the body that has governed Gaza since 2007 and handing over control to a technocratic committee — the National Committee for the Administration of Gaza — established under Trump's Board of Peace as part of an October 2025 ceasefire deal.

The Guardian also reported that Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu urged the US not to sell F-35 jets or components to Turkey, arguing it would upset the power balance in the region, as Trump traveled to Ankara for a NATO summit. Separately, the outlet reported an Israeli drone strike on a car in south Lebanon killed four people including three women, despite a fragile Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire. Israel has maintained intermittent strikes on south Lebanon, citing targeting of Hezbollah sites.
